prevalence.value: ~0.2-1.0 per 100,000/year (higher in occupational and recreational exposure settings)
prevalence.trend: stable; localized increases after flooding and in outdoor recreational exposure

burden.mortalityRate: Low; higher in severe Weil syndrome

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical suspicion plus serology (IgM, MAT) and/or PCR; case definitions per ECDC

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: no screening; tested based on exposure history and compatible illness
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with early treatment; severe cases require hospital support

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Access to serology and PCR in most member states; occupational and recreational exposure history emphasized
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Doxycycline for mild disease; IV penicillin/ceftriaxone for severe disease; supportive renal/hepatic care
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Low clinical suspicion outside endemic micro-areas and increasing flood-related exposure events
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Strong laboratory networks and occupational health awareness in many member states
